title: Shukar Chalisa
intro: Shri Shukra Chalisa: Full Path, Meaning, and Benefits - Hymn to Lord Shukra
description:
|| Shri Shukra Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Shri Ganpati guru gauri, Shankar Hanumat kinha.
Binvaun shubh phal den Hari, mud mangal deen.||

|| Chaupai ||
Jayati jayati Shukra dev dayala.
Karat sada janpratipada.||
Shwetambar, shwet vaaran, shobhit.
Mukh mand, chandan hiya lobhit.||
Sundar ratnajalit aabhushan.
Priyahin madhur, sheetal suvaasan.||
Sapt bhuj, sobha nidhi lavanya.
Karat sada jan, mangal kaanya.||
Mangalmay, sukh sada savaarath.
Deendayalu, kripa nidhi paarath.||
Shubhra swachh, Ganga jal jaisa.
Darshan se, harshay manaisa.||
Tribhuvan, maha mangal kaari.
Deenan hit, kripa nidhi saari.||
Dev daanav, rishi muni bhaktan.
Kasht mitavan, bhanjan jagatan.||
Mohbaari, manhar hiyara.
Sarv vidhi sukh, saukhya phulaara.||
Karat krodh, chapal bhuj dhaari.
Kasht nivaran, sant dukhari.||
Shubhra varn, tanu mand suhaana.
Kasht mitavan, harshit naana.||
Dusht haran, sujanan hitkaari.
Sarv baadha, nivaran nyaari.||
Sur patihin, prabhu kripa vilaasin.
Kasht nivaran, shubhra suvaasin.||
Ved puran, pathat jan swami.
Manharan, mohbaari kaami.||
Sapt bhuj, ratnajalit mala.
Kasht nivaran, shubh phalshaala.||
Sukh rakshak, sarvsukh daata.
Sarv kaamana, phal daata.||
Manav krit, paap hare prabhu.
Sarv baadha, nivaran raghu.||
Rog nivaran, dukh harankar.
Sarv vidhi, shubh phal denekar.||
Naman sakal, sur nar muni karte.
Vrat upasak, dukh haran karte.||
Sharanagat, kripa nidhi soi.
Jan rakshak, mohe dukh hoi.||
Shuddh bhaav se jo nit gaavai.
Sarv sukh, param pad paavai.||
Vrindavan mein, mandir nirmit.
Jahaan shuddh bhaktan, sada sharanagat.||
Sant janan ke, kasht mitavat.
Bhavbandhan se, sahaj chhudavat.||
Sakal kaamana, purn karaavat.
Mohbhang, bhavsaagar taraavat.||
Jayati jayati, kripaanidhaan.
Shukra dev, shri vishwa viddhaan.||
Pranvaun, naath sakal gun saagar.
Vividh vighn haran, sukhdaayak.||
Sur muni janan, ati priya swami.
Shubhra varn, roop manhaari.||
Jai jai jai, shri Shukra dayaala.
Karahun kripa, bhav bandhan taala.||
Dhyaan dharat, jan houm sukhaari.
Kripa drishti, shaanti hitkaari.||
Adham kaayar, subuddhi sudhaaro.
Moh nivaran, kasht nivaaro.||
Lakshmipati, shubh phal daata.
Santjanan, dukh bhanjan raata.||
Jai jai jai, kripa nidhi Shukra.
Karahun kripa, harahun sab du:kh.||
Pranvaun naath, sakal gun saagar.
Vividh vighn haran, sukhdaayak.||
Roop tej bal, sampan sada.
Shaanti daayak, jan sukh daata.||
Tribhuvan mein, mangal kartoo.
Sarv baadha, harta Shukru.||
Manav krit, paap hare prabhu.
Sarv baadha, nivaran raghu.||
Rog nivaran, dukh harankar.
Sarv vidhi, shubh phal denekar.||
Pranvaun naath, sakal gun saagar.
Vividh vighn haran, sukhdaayak.||
Dhyaan dharat, jan houm sukhaari.
Kripa drishti, shaanti hitkaari.||
Jai jai jai, kripa nidhi Shukra.
Karahun kripa, harahun sab du:kh.||

|| Doha ||
Namo namo shri Shukra suhaave.
Sarv baadha, kasht mitaave.||
Yeh chalisa, jo nit gaavai.
Sukh sampatti, param pad paavai.||
|| Iti sampurnam ||
meaning:
|| Shri Shukra Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Shri Ganpati guru Gauri, Shankar Hanumat kinha.
(Salutations to Shri Ganpati, Guru, Mother Gauri, Shankar, and Hanuman.)
Binvaun shubh phal den Hari, mud mangal deen.||
(I pray to the Lord to grant auspicious results and bestow joy and well-being.)

|| Chaupai ||
Jayati jayati Shukra dev dayala, karat sada janpratipada.||
(Victory to you, O merciful Lord Shukra; you always protect the people.)
Shwetambar, shwet vaaran, shobhit, mukh mand, chandan hiya lobhit.||
(Clad in white, fair-complexioned and radiant; your gentle face and sandalwood on your heart are enchanting.)
Sundar ratnajalit aabhushan, priyahin madhur, sheetal suvaasan.||
(Adorned with beautiful gem-studded ornaments, you are fond of sweet and cooling fragrances.)
Sapt bhuj, sobha nidhi lavanya, karat sada jan, mangal kaanya.||
(You are the seven-armed repository of beauty; you always bring auspiciousness to the people.)
Mangalmay, sukh sada savaarath, deendayalu, kripa nidhi paarath.||
(You are auspicious, the eternal provider of happiness, merciful to the poor, and an ocean of grace.)
Shubhra swachh, Ganga jal jaisa, darshan se, harshay manaisa.||
(You are pure like the waters of the Ganges; the mind rejoices upon beholding you.)
Tribhuvan, maha mangal kaari, deenan hit, kripa nidhi saari.||
(You are the great auspicious one of the three worlds and a complete ocean of grace for the distressed.)
Dev daanav, rishi muni bhaktan, kasht mitavan, bhanjan jagatan.||
(You remove the sufferings of gods, demons, sages, and devotees, shattering the worldly pains.)
Mohbaari, manhar hiyara, sarv vidhi sukh, saukhya phulaara.||
(You cut through the webs of attachment; your heart is captivating, and you provide joy and happiness in every way.)
Karat krodh, chapal bhuj dhaari, kasht nivaran, sant dukhari.||
(Possessing agile arms, you remove the troubles of sorrowful saints.)
Shubhra varn, tanu mand suhaana, kasht mitavan, harshit naana.||
(With a fair complexion and a pleasant body, you remove diverse pains and bring joy.)
Dusht haran, sujanan hitkaari, sarv baadha, nivaran nyaari.||
(You are the destroyer of the wicked and the well-wisher of the virtuous; your way of removing all obstacles is unique.)
Sur patihin, prabhu kripa vilaasin, kasht nivaran, shubhra suvaasin.||
(You shower your grace upon the King of Gods; you are the remover of troubles and filled with pure fragrance.)
Ved puran, pathat jan swami, manharan, mohbaari kaami.||
(The Vedas and Puranas sing of your lordship; you are captivating and cut through worldly desires.)
Sapt bhuj, ratnajalit mala, kasht nivaran, shubh phalshaala.||
(With seven arms and wearing a gem-studded garland, you remove suffering and are a house of auspicious fruits.)
Sukh rakshak, sarvsukh daata, sarv kaamana, phal daata.||
(You are the protector of happiness, the giver of all joys, and the fulfiller of all desires.)
Manav krit, paap hare prabhu, sarv baadha, nivaran raghu.||
(O Lord, you remove the sins committed by humans and eliminate all obstacles.)
Rog nivaran, dukh harankar, sarv vidhi, shubh phal denekar.||
(You are the healer of diseases, the remover of sorrows, and the provider of auspicious fruits in all ways.)
Naman sakal, sur nar muni karte, vrat upasak, dukh haran karte.||
(All gods, humans, and sages bow to you; you remove the sorrows of those who fast and worship you.)
Sharanagat, kripa nidhi soi, jan rakshak, mohe dukh hoi.||
(You are the ocean of grace for those who surrender; you are the protector of people; please remove my sorrow.)
Shuddh bhaav se jo nit gaavai, sarv sukh, param pad paavai.||
(Whoever sings this daily with a pure heart attains all happiness and the supreme state.)
Vrindavan mein, mandir nirmit, jahaan shuddh bhaktan, sada sharanagat.||
(Your temple is built in Vrindavan, where pure devotees are always surrendered to you.)
Sant janan ke, kasht mitavat, bhavbandhan se, sahaj chhudavat.||
(You erase the troubles of saints and easily liberate them from the bonds of the world.)
Sakal kaamana, purn karaavat, mohbhang, bhavsaagar taraavat.||
(You fulfill all desires, break the illusions of attachment, and help cross the ocean of existence.)
Jayati jayati, kripaanidhaan, Shukra dev, shri vishwa viddhaan.||
(Victory to you, O treasure of grace, Lord Shukra, the scholar of the world.)
Pranvaun, naath sakal gun saagar, vividh vighn haran, sukhdaayak.||
(I bow to you, O Lord, ocean of all virtues, remover of diverse obstacles, and bestower of happiness.)
Sur muni janan, ati priya swami, shubhra varn, roop manhaari.||
(You are the beloved Lord of gods and sages; your fair complexion and form are enchanting.)
Jai jai jai, shri Shukra dayaala, karahun kripa, bhav bandhan taala.||
(Victory to you, O merciful Lord Shukra; shower your grace and unlock the bonds of existence.)
Dhyaan dharat, jan houm sukhaari, kripa drishti, shaanti hitkaari.||
(By meditating on you, people become happy; your gaze of grace is the bringer of peace.)
Adham kaayar, subuddhi sudhaaro, moh nivaran, kasht nivaaro.||
(I am cowardly and low; please improve my intellect, remove my attachments, and resolve my troubles.)
Lakshmipati, shubh phal daata, santjanan, dukh bhanjan raata.||
(O Lord of Lakshmi, giver of auspicious results, you are the destroyer of the sorrows of saints.)
Jai jai jai, kripa nidhi Shukra, karahun kripa, harahun sab du:kh.||
(Victory to you, O ocean of grace Shukra; shower your grace and remove all my sorrows.)

|| Doha ||
Namo namo shri Shukra suhaave, sarv baadha, kasht mitaave.||
(Salutations again and again to the beautiful Lord Shukra; please erase all obstacles and troubles.)
Yeh chalisa, jo nit gaavai, sukh sampatti, param pad paavai.||
(Whoever sings this Chalisa daily attains happiness, wealth, and the supreme state.)
|| Iti sampurnam ||

faq.q1: Why is the Shukra Chalisa recited?
faq.a1: Lord Shukra (Venus) is the significator of luxury, comfort, art, and love. This Chalisa is recited to attain material happiness, opulence, harmony in married life, and magnetic charm.
faq.q2: What is the most auspicious day to recite the Shukra Chalisa?
faq.a2: Friday (Shukravar) is dedicated to the worship of Shukra Dev. Therefore, reciting it on a Friday is considered the most auspicious and rewarding.
faq.q3: Does this Chalisa improve married life?
faq.a3: Yes, Lord Shukra is the deity of love and marital bliss. Regular recitation is highly effective in resolving tensions between couples and fostering deeper affection.
faq.q4: Does the Shukra Chalisa help in improving financial status?
faq.a4: Absolutely. In astrology, Venus is the significator of luxury and wealth. Its blessings help an individual attain pleasant material resources and financial prosperity in life.
faq.q5: Is any special material required for it?
faq.a5: Shukra Dev favors fragrant items and the color white. Offering jasmine or white flowers and using fragrant perfume or incense during the recitation is helpful in receiving his blessings quickly.
